paragraphs.text: Student of Jean Monnet Modules Derecho project: Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers Harmonisation, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. As a third law student, this year I am learning a variety of new things about the right to harm and consumption that you did not know before. This has helped me to tell me many things that I had previously overlooked but which in reality today seem to me to be a real problem as I have realised that there are many patterns in this area that influence my way of acting as a consumer and I believe that there is an increasing need for more stringent regulations or regulations on this issue. That is why the law on digital fairness seems to me to be a very correct and necessary initiative. In my view, such legislation should, in my view, focus primarily on minors. Today, we see how minors have access to the internet from very small numbers and there are many situations in which they are unprotected, so that this does not happen, it will be necessary to address certain risks that seem to be solved in some way, but which in practice are not. For the time being, measures such as the minimum age for registration, the parental ban on the use of certain applications, the privacy configuration seem to me to be necessary. However, these measures are not sufficient as we know that there are many ways in which children can continue to make use of all kinds of platforms without any consequence. That is why I think it is of utmost importance and need to improve this type of practice by introducing stricter regulation so that children can have a healthy and safe relationship with digital issues, online shopping, video games, etc.
